b:T27280,<div class="specification">
<p>Two models of plasma membrane structure are shown.</p>
<p style="text-align: center;"><img src="data:image/png;base64,"/></p>
<p style="text-align: center;">[Source: diagram from article published in <em>The American Journal of Pathology</em>, <strong>65</strong>, J Singer and G Nicolson,<br/>The structure and chemistry of mammalian cell membranes, 427–437, Copyright Elsevier (1971)]</p>
</div><div class="specification">
<p>Phospholipase C is an enzyme that digests the polar heads of phospholipids. Scientists used phospholipase C to test these models of membrane structure. They found that the enzyme could digest the heads of phospholipids in the plasma membranes of red blood cells.</p>
</div><div class="question" style="padding-left: 20px; padding-right: 20px;">
<p>State the scientists who proposed model A.</p>
<div class="marks">[1]</div>
<div class="question_part_label">a.</div>
</div><div class="question" style="padding-left: 20px; padding-right: 20px;">
<p>Label the model A diagram to show a region of protein.</p>
<div class="marks">[1]</div>
<div class="question_part_label">b.i.</div>
</div><div class="question" style="padding-left: 20px; padding-right: 20px;">
<p>Label the model B diagram to show a phospholipid.</p>
<div class="marks">[1]</div>
<div class="question_part_label">b.ii.</div>
</div><div class="question" style="padding-left: 20px; padding-right: 20px;">
<p>Deduce <strong>one</strong> conclusion about the structure of the plasma membrane reached by the scientists from their results.</p>
<div class="marks">[1]</div>
<div class="question_part_label">c.i.</div>
</div><div class="question" style="padding-left: 20px; padding-right: 20px;">
<p>Suggest <strong>one</strong> reason for maintaining a pH of 7.5 throughout the experiment.</p>
<div class="marks">[2]</div>
<div class="question_part_label">c.ii.</div>
</div><div class="question" style="padding-left: 20px; padding-right: 20px;">
<p>State <strong>one</strong> technological improvement, other than enzymatic digestion, that led to the falsification of previous models to determine the current model of membrane structure.</p>
<div class="marks">[1]</div>
<div class="question_part_label">d.</div>
</div>c:Tf8e6,<div class="question" style="padding-left: 20px;">
<p>Davson–Danielli ✔</p>
<div class="question_part_label">a.</div>
</div><div class="question" style="padding-left: 20px;">
<p><img src="data:image/png;base64,"/></p>
<p>[Source: diagram from article published in <em>The American Journal of Pathology</em>, <strong>65</strong>, J Singer and G Nicolson, The structure and chemistry of mammalian cell membranes, 427–437, Copyright Elsevier (1971)]</p>
<p><em>Accept label to top protein.</em></p>
<div class="question_part_label">b.i.</div>
</div><div class="question" style="padding-left: 20px;">
<p><img src="data:image/png;base64,"/></p>
<p>[Source: diagram from article published in <em>The American Journal of Pathology</em>, <strong>65</strong>, J Singer and G Nicolson, The structure and chemistry of mammalian cell membranes, 427–437, Copyright Elsevier (1971)]</p>
<p><em>Accept a label to any part of any phospholipid</em><br/><em>Accept different form of labelling that clearly indicate the phospholipids.</em></p>
<div class="question_part_label">b.ii.</div>
</div><div class="question" style="padding-left: 20px;">
<p>a. phospholipids on outside/exposed ✔</p>
<p>b. impossibility of continuous protein layer «of Davson–Danielli/model A» ✔</p>
<p>c. supports idea of mosaic pattern of membrane<br/><em><strong>OR</strong></em><br/>supports model B ✔</p>
<p><em>Award any other valid conclusion. </em></p>
<p><em>Do not accept "membrane is made of phospholipids".</em></p>
<div class="question_part_label">c.i.</div>
</div><div class="question" style="padding-left: 20px;">
<p>a. pH values away from optimum pH affect enzyme<br/><em><strong>OR</strong></em><br/>so enzyme can function properly<br/><em><strong>OR</strong></em><br/>pH 7.5 is the optimum pH for the enzyme ✔<br/><br/>b. sketch of enzyme activity versus pH ✔ <em>Sketch of enzyme activity needs labels.</em></p>
<p>c. change in pH affects 3D structure of protein/active site<br/><em><strong>OR</strong></em><br/>change in pH denatures the enzyme / protein ✔</p>
<p>d. substrate does not fit in active site<br/><em><strong>OR</strong></em><br/>interaction of substrate and active site affected ✔</p>
<div class="question_part_label">c.ii.</div>
</div><div class="question" style="padding-left: 20px;">
<p>a. scanning electronmicrography / SEM ✔</p>
<p>b. freeze fracture/etching ✔</p>
<p>c. X-ray diffraction<br/><em><strong>OR</strong></em><br/>crystallography ✔</p>
<p>d. fluorescent antibody / marker tagging ✔</p>
<p><em>Do not accept electron microscope</em></p>
<p><em>Accept description of process</em></p>
<div class="question_part_label">d.</div>
</div>d:T583,<div class="question" style="padding-left: 20px;">
<p>There were a variety of suggestions for the names of the scientists with Singer and Nicolson, as given in the question paper, being a common incorrect answer.</p>
<div class="question_part_label">a.</div>
</div><div class="question" style="padding-left: 20px;">
<p>Most students could label the areas with protein and phospholipid.</p>
<div class="question_part_label">b.i.</div>
</div><div class="question" style="padding-left: 20px;">
<p>Most students could label the areas with protein and phospholipid.</p>
<div class="question_part_label">b.ii.</div>
</div><div class="question" style="padding-left: 20px;">
<p>Many candidates answered that the enzyme acted on the phospholipids but failed to make a deduction about the structure of the membrane.</p>
<div class="question_part_label">c.i.</div>
</div><div class="question" style="padding-left: 20px;">
<p>Most candidates answered correctly that a specific pH was necessary for the enzyme to function properly and that deviation from this pH could denature the enzyme.</p>
<div class="question_part_label">c.ii.</div>
</div><div class="question" style="padding-left: 20px;">
<p>Stating a technological improvement that led to the currently accepted model of the cell membrane proved more challenging and only the better candidates answered correctly.</p>
<div class="question_part_label">d.</div>
</div>2:["$","article",null,{"className":"q-page","children":[["$","p",null,{"style":{"margin":0},"children":["$","$L6",null,{"href":"/subject/biology/topic/1/","children":["← back to ","Biology"," topic ","1"]}]}],["$","h1",null,{"children":"19M.3.SL.TZ2.1"}],["$","div",null,{"className":"q-meta","children":[["$","span",null,{"className":"tag","children":"pestle"}],["$","span",null,{"children":"Biology"}],null,["$","span",null,{"children":"SL"}],["$","span",null,{"children":["Paper ","3"]}],["$","span",null,{"children":"19M"}],["$","span",null,{"children":["· ","1-3-membrane-structure"]}],["$","a",null,{"href":"https://pestle.pages.dev/app/","target":"_blank","rel":"noopener","children":"source ↗"}]]}],["$","div",null,{"className":"q-content","dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"$b"}}],["$","details",null,{"className":"solution","children":[["$","summary",null,{"children":"Markscheme / solution"}],["$","div",null,{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"$c"}}]]}],["$","details",null,{"className":"solution","children":[["$","summary",null,{"children":"Examiners’ report"}],["$","div",null,{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"$d"}}]]}]]}]
